notes: Roman general known for his shrewd military leadership and temperate character, notably in the war against Perseus.

docs.title: Roman History, Book Book 20
docs.author: Cassius Dio
docs.excerpt: For example, he did not assume a pompous or boastful attitude toward Perseus, nor had he been careless or incautious in his conduct of the war against him. He was not only good at general ­ship, but he scorned money.

docs.title: Life of Aemilius Paulus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: Nor was he less severe, in requiring and observing the ancient Roman discipline in military affairs; not endeavoring, when he had the command, to ingratiate himself with his soldiers by popular flattery, though this custom prevailed at that time amongst many, who, by
